verdict: 2026-07-12 16:30 PDT — The overall client-only simplification is modern, cohesive, and consistent with the repository: React useState is sufficient, existing pure cost/cadence helpers remain appropriate, and no dependency offers a meaningfully simpler solution. I would build it substantially this way, but the sketch has two boundary omissions that should be resolved before implementation.

findings.title: Participant removal can silently weaken the retained base-video pipeline
findings.claim: The sketch removes the participant refresh/list while promising that drawParticipant remains unchanged. RealZoom currently obtains _selfUUID from getUserContext first but explicitly falls back to matching the presenter against the refreshed participant list. Removing that list without replacing or proving the fallback unnecessary can make drawCameraOverlay skip drawParticipant, contradicting the acceptance criterion that the base-video pipeline remains unchanged.
findings.alternative: State and test the new self-UUID contract explicitly: normalize a guaranteed participant UUID from getUserContext during init, or identify another non-list SDK source. If the Zoom SDK does not guarantee that identifier, revise the scope because complete removal of getMeetingParticipants and preservation of base-video drawing are not simultaneously supported by the current design.
findings.win: Preserves the existing camera base layer and removes an otherwise silent production-only regression path while still eliminating participant tracking from application state.

findings.title: Client identity capability is left behind after persistence removal
findings.claim: The session-only sketch removes ratesApi and all store calls but does not say to remove zoomAdapter.getAppContext or the getAppContext capability. That leaves a dead client-side identity/persistence seam after Story 1, so the claimed client-first/server-later boundary is incomplete even though the server store itself remains correctly untouched.
findings.alternative: Remove getAppContext from both adapter implementations, ZOOM_CAPABILITIES, adapter documentation, and affected client tests in Story 1. Leave server identity, rate-store routes, Marketplace configuration, and scope documentation untouched for Story 2 as specified.
findings.win: Eliminates the final client API and requested SDK capability used solely by the retired rate-store flow, making the server store genuinely unused without expanding into deferred server or Marketplace work.
